name: hr-pro
description: Professional, ethical HR partner for hiring,
capabilities: Generate structured interview kits with rubrics and scorecards for any role
Create compliant job descriptions with inclusive language and EOE statements
Build 30/60/90 day onboarding plans with checklists and stakeholder mapping
Draft PTO and leave policies with accrual formulas and jurisdiction-specific notes
Design performance improvement plans (PIPs) with objective coaching criteria
Create employee relations documentation templates for investigations and conflict resolution
Generate offboarding checklists and exit interview guides
Provide competency matrices and goal-setting frameworks by role level
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Provides HR guidance with proper legal disclaimers and ethical guardrails. No security concerns detected. Static analysis scanned 0 files (prompt-only).
valueStatement: HR professionals and managers spend hours creating compliant policies, interview kits, and performance documents from scratch. This skill provides instant, jurisdiction-aware templates and structured guidance for hiring, onboarding, PTO management, performance reviews, and employee relations.
userTitle: Streamline HR Workflows with Professional Templates
limitations: Not a substitute for licensed legal advice - requires counsel consultation for high-risk actions
Jurisdiction-specific rules may require local legal review before implementation
Cannot provide real-time updates on changing labor laws or regulations
Templates require customization with company-specific details and local compliance checks

useCases.targetUser: Startup Founder Building First Hiring Process
useCases.title: Startup Founder Building First Hiring Process
useCases.description: Early-stage founders need to establish professional hiring practices from scratch. Use this skill to create job descriptions, structured interview kits, and onboarding plans that scale with the company.

useCases.title: HR Manager Managing Performance Reviews
useCases.description: HR professionals leading quarterly or annual review cycles can generate competency matrices, goal-setting frameworks, calibration guidelines, and review packet templates for consistent evaluations.

useCases.title: People Operations Director Expanding Internationally
useCases.description: Companies entering new jurisdictions need jurisdiction-aware policy templates for PTO, leave, and compliance. This skill provides neutral frameworks with prompts for local counsel review.

promptTemplates.title: Create a Job Description
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive job description for a {{RoleTitle}} at {{CompanyName}}. Include mission statement, 90-day outcomes, core competencies, required vs. preferred qualifications, pay range context, and an inclusive equal opportunity employer statement.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when opening a new role or revising existing job descriptions to ensure clarity and inclusivity.

promptTemplates.title: Build Structured Interview Kit
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a structured interview kit for {{RoleTitle}} including 10-12 job-related questions (behavioral, situational, technical), a 1-5 scoring rubric with defined anchors for each competency, panel interviewer assignments, and a scorecard template.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when preparing for candidate interviews to ensure consistent, bias-mitigated evaluations.

promptTemplates.title: Design Onboarding Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 30/60/90 day onboarding plan for a new {{RoleTitle}} joining {{Department}}. Include learning objectives, key stakeholder meetings, deliverables for each milestone, IT/provisioning checklist, and buddy program outline.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when welcoming new hires to ensure smooth integration and clear expectations.

promptTemplates.title: Draft Performance Improvement Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft a performance improvement plan for a {{RoleTitle}} with specific performance gaps to address. Include objective SMART goals, coaching support steps, timeline (30-60-90 days), success criteria, and documentation requirements. Note: This is not legal advice.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when managing performance issues with a coaching-focused, documentation-rich approach.

bestPractices: Always specify your jurisdiction and company context when requesting HR documents, as labor laws vary significantly by location
Use structured interview templates with rubrics to reduce bias and ensure consistent candidate evaluations
Consult qualified legal counsel before implementing policies involving terminations, protected leave, or cross-border employment
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill for legal advice - it provides general HR information and requires counsel review for high-risk actions
Avoid implementing policies without jurisdiction-specific review, especially for international operations or unionized workplaces
Do not request or collect sensitive personal data beyond what is necessary for the stated HR purpose

faq.question: Is this skill a substitute for legal advice?
faq.answer: No. HR-Pro provides general HR information and templates only. Consult qualified local legal counsel before implementing policies or taking actions with legal effect, especially for terminations, protected leave, immigration, or international operations.

faq.question: What jurisdiction should I specify?
faq.answer: Provide your country, state/province, and region. Labor laws vary significantly at all levels. If you are unsure, provide your best estimate and the skill will provide jurisdiction-neutral guidance with notes on what to verify locally.

faq.question: Can I use these templates for international employees?
faq.answer: Templates include jurisdiction-neutral frameworks with prompts for local review. For international or cross-border scenarios, always engage local counsel in each jurisdiction before implementation, as employment laws vary widely.

faq.question: How do I customize the templates for my company?
faq.answer: Templates use placeholders like {{CompanyName}}, {{RoleTitle}}, and {{Jurisdiction}}. Replace these with your specific details. Review against your internal policies, culture, and legal requirements before use.

faq.question: What information should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Include your jurisdiction, company size, industry, employment types (full-time/contractor), work model (remote/hybrid/on-site), union status, and any internal policy constraints.

faq.question: Are the interview rubrics compliant with bias and discrimination laws?
faq.answer: Templates use job-related, objective criteria designed to mitigate bias. However, you should validate rubrics against local anti-discrimination laws and ensure questions focus on job-related qualifications without prohibited inquiries.
